reason: CD-ROM = Compact Disk Read Only Memory; other expansions are invented.
ALU = Arithmetic Logic Unit, the CPU unit doing arithmetic and logic operations.
VGA = Video Graphics Array (the 'A' in VGA/SVGA is Array; MDA/CGA use Adapter).
IBM 1401 (1959) is a transistor-based machine, i.e. second generation.
MSI = Medium Scale Integration / Medium Scale Integrated circuits.
Standard 3.5-inch high-density floppy holds 1.44 MB.
The first computer brought to Nepal (1971, for the census) was an IBM 1401.
MICR = Magnetic Ink Character Reader/Recognition, used for cheque processing.
EBCDIC = Extended Binary Coded Decimal Interchange Code (IBM 8-bit code).
BCD = Binary Coded Decimal.
ASCII = American Standard Code for Information Interchange.
EDSAC (1949, vacuum tubes) is first generation; IBM-1401 and CDC-1604 are second gen, ICL-2900 fourth.
First generation computers used vacuum tubes/valves; transistors are second gen, ICs third.
FORTRAN = FORmula TRANslation.
EEPROM = Electrically Erasable Programmable Read Only Memory.
Transistor era conventionally dated ~1956-1965; 1949-1955 is first gen, later ranges are third/fourth.
Vacuum-tube first-generation machines were room-sized; size shrank with each later generation.
Microprocessors (Intel 4004, 1971) define the fourth generation.
OCR scans and recognises ordinary printed text; OMR reads marks, MICR reads magnetic-ink characters only.
Printer resolution/quality is measured in DPI = dots per inch, a linear measure; 'dots per square inch' is not a real printing metric (speed, option C, is PPM not quality).
